Hi Diogo, this is Sophia. Next week you will give your first short presentation to the leadership team about Project Atlas. Let me give you a quick brief on how to structure it. Start with a clear opening. Welcome the audience, say your name, and give the topic. Then share an overview of your outline, so people know what is coming. Use signpost words to guide them. First, present the problem. Then, show your solution. Next, give the timeline. After that, talk about the budget. Finally, end with the next steps. Keep it simple and confident. You do not need many slides, just a clear structure. You have learned to run a whole meeting, so a short presentation is the natural next step. You will do great. Talk soon.

Good morning everyone, and thank you for coming. My name is Diogo Leal, and I am the project manager for Project Atlas. Today my topic is our plan for the new feature launch. Let me give you a quick overview of the outline. First, I will present the main problem we want to solve. Then, I will show you our proposed solution. Next, I will walk you through the timeline. After that, I will talk about the budget. Finally, I will end with the next steps and answer your questions. There are five short parts, so this will be quick and clear. Let us begin with the problem.
